revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''松ヶ崎横穴墓群'''（まつがさきよこあなぼぐん）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[港南区]][[港南台]]9丁目にある古墳時…」
revision.text:
'''松ヶ崎横穴墓群'''（まつがさきよこあなぼぐん）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[港南区]][[港南台]]9丁目にある[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]初めごろの[[横穴墓群|横穴墓群遺跡]]。[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]]の1つ。
==概要== [[神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]]（かつて[[神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]）南側敷地の山の斜面にある。行政区は港南区だが[[栄区]]との区界に面し、[[㹨川]]の谷を降りた位置にあるため地域的にもほぼ栄区である。かつては8基残っていたといわれるが、現場は雑木林でほとんど草木や土に埋められ、横穴の入口が少しだけ見えるような状態で2-3基が解る程度である。
[[羨道]]（入口）奥の死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と[[蒲鉾]]形天井をもっており、さらに奥の壁に「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小部屋を掘り造る「[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（[[鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]」というデザインが特徴<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>。
横浜市に「登録地域文化財」に指定されているが、表示になるようなものはなにもない<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=タウンニュース港南区・栄区版|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>。
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台-横浜市港南台土地区画整理事業にともなう調査-（神奈川県埋蔵文化財調査報告9）』1976年（昭和51年）3月発行 *港南の歴史発刊実行委員会『港南の歴史』1979年（昭和54年）10月1日発行
==所在地== {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E}}}}
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==関連項目== *[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓） *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] *[[七石山横穴墓群]] *[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]]
==外部リンク== *{{Cite web|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=松ケ崎横穴古墳群の現状|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{デフォルトソート:まつかさきよこあなほくん}} [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:港南区の歴史]]

'''松ヶ崎横穴墓群'''（まつがさきよこあなぼぐん）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[港南区]][[港南台]]9丁目にある[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]初めごろの[[横穴墓群|横穴墓群遺跡]]。[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]]の1つ。[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域史跡]]に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=タウンニュース港南区・栄区版|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>。
==概要== [[神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]]（かつて[[神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]）南側敷地の山の斜面にある。行政区は港南区だが[[栄区]]との区界に面し、[[㹨川]]の谷を降りた位置にあるため地域的にもほぼ栄区である。かつては8基残っていたといわれるが、現場は雑木林でほとんど草木や土に埋められ、横穴の入口が少しだけ見えるような状態で2-3基が解る程度である。
[[羨道]]（入口）奥の死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と[[蒲鉾]]形天井をもっており、さらに奥の壁に「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小部屋を掘り造る「[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（[[鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]」というデザインが特徴<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>。
2015年（平成27年）11月13日に[[横浜市登録地域文化財]]の「地域史跡」に登録されている<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web |title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>。
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台-横浜市港南台土地区画整理事業にともなう調査-（神奈川県埋蔵文化財調査報告9）』1976年（昭和51年）3月発行 *港南の歴史発刊実行委員会『港南の歴史』1979年（昭和54年）10月1日発行
==所在地== {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E}}}}
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==関連項目== *[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓） *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] *[[七石山横穴墓群]] *[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]] *[[横浜市登録地域文化財]] *[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]

'''松ヶ崎横穴墓群'''（まつがさきよこあなぼぐん）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[港南区]][[港南台]]9丁目にある[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]初めごろの[[横穴墓群|横穴墓群遺跡]]。[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]]の1つ。[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域史跡]]に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=タウンニュース港南区・栄区版|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>。

[[羨道]]（入口）奥の死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と[[蒲鉾]]形天井をもっており、さらに奥の壁に「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小部屋を掘り造る「[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（[[鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]」というデザインが特徴<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>。
2015年（平成27年）11月13日に[[横浜市登録地域文化財]]の「地域史跡」に登録されている<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web |title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>。

==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台-横浜市港南台土地区画整理事業にともなう調査-（神奈川県埋蔵文化財調査報告9）』1976年（昭和51年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==関連項目== *[[港南台遺跡群]] *[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓） *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] *[[七石山横穴墓群]] *[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]] *[[横浜市登録地域文化財]] *[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]
==外部リンク== *{{Cite web|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=松ケ崎横穴古墳群の現状|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{日本の文化財}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}} {{デフォルトソート:まつかさきよこあなほくん}} [[Category:神奈川県の考古遺跡]] [[Category:港南区の歴史]] [[Category:横穴墓]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:神奈川県の古墳]] [[Category:神奈川県内の市町村指定史跡]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|松ヶ崎横穴墓群の1基。藪に埋もれた横穴入口がほんの少し顔を覗かせている（2020年5月5日撮影）。|サムネイル]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230|coord={{coord2|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E}}}}
'''松ヶ崎横穴墓群'''（まつがさきよこあなぼぐん）は[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]][[港南区]][[港南台]]9丁目にある[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]初めごろの[[横穴墓群|横穴墓群遺跡]]。[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]]の1つ。[[横浜市登録地域文化財|横浜市登録地域史跡]]に登録されている<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=タウンニュース港南区・栄区版|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>。
==概要== [[神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校]]（かつて[[神奈川県立港南台高等学校]]）南側敷地の山の斜面にある。行政区は港南区だが[[栄区]]との区界に面し、[[㹨川]]の谷を降りた位置にあるため地域的にもほぼ栄区である。かつて[[港南台遺跡群]]に数えられ、8基残っていたといわれるが<ref>神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台』（1976年）156-158ページ</ref>、現場は雑木林でほとんど草木や土に埋められ、横穴の入口が少しだけ見えるような状態で2-3基が解る程度である。
[[羨道]]（入口）奥の死者の安置場（[[玄室]]）が、細長い台形の床面と[[蒲鉾]]形天井をもっており、さらに奥の壁に「棺室」といわれる置き棚のような小部屋を掘り造る「[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（[[鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓]]」というデザインが特徴<ref>埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）『埋文よこはま31』（2015年）1-3ページ</ref>。
2015年（平成27年）11月13日に[[横浜市登録地域文化財]]の「地域史跡」に登録されている<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web |title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher=[[横浜市教育委員会]]|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>。
==詳しく載っている本== *[[鵠沼女子高等学校]]地歴研究部 『横浜市戸塚区㹨川流域の横穴群について』1964年（昭和39年）3月20日発行 *神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台-横浜市港南台土地区画整理事業にともなう調査-（神奈川県埋蔵文化財調査報告9）』1976年（昭和51年）3月発行 *港南の歴史発刊実行委員会『港南の歴史』1979年（昭和54年）10月1日発行
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課『港南台-横浜市港南台土地区画整理事業にともなう調査-（神奈川県埋蔵文化財調査報告9）』1976年（昭和51年）3月発行 *[[埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|埋蔵文化財センター（横浜市）]]『[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf 埋文よこはま31号（㹨川流域の横穴墓群）]』2015年（平成27年）3月20日発行
==関連項目== *[[港南台遺跡群]] *[[鎌倉型横穴墓]]（鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓） *[[㹨川流域横穴墓群]] *[[七石山横穴墓群]] *[[宮ノ前横穴墓群]] *[[横浜市登録地域文化財]] *[[横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧]]
==外部リンク== *{{Cite web|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=松ケ崎横穴古墳群の現状|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

Bot作業依頼#Cite webの和書引数追加

Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster)]'' (March 20, 2015).

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Kashio River Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}
[[Category:Kofun in Yokohama]] [[Category:History of Kōnan Ward, Yokohama]] [[Category:Cave tombs]] [[Category:Cave tomb clusters]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites of Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]]
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster]] to [[Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]]
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ''' {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ''' {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ''' {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ''' {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachikawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
