revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Japanese burial practices]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster-qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rock-cut tomb cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[ja:横穴墓]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun forks]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster-qq]] [[Category:kk]]
{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:11]]

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).

== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
