revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=draft}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=draft}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区|12=draft}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=draft}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=draft}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=draft}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=draft}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=draft}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=draft}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=draft}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=draft}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=draft}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=draft}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区|12=draft}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=draft}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=draft}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=draft}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=draft}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=draft}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=draft}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=draft}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧|12=draft}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|qq=|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|qq=|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|qq=|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|qq=|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|qq=|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|qq=|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|qq=|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|qq=|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|qq=|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|qq=|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|qq=|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|qq=|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|qq=|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|1=List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543021}}
== External Links == *{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://teishoin.net/blog/003984.html|title=Current State of the Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|website=Kameno's Digital Photo Log|date=2010-09-22|accessdate=2020-07-22}}
{{Japanese Cultural Properties}} {{coord|35|22|09.1|N|139|34|21.2|E|region:JP-14|display=title}}
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb Tomb concentration) == ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * [[:en:Hōōzan Cave Tombs|Hōōzan Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Cave Tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]])

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * {{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''[https://www.rekihaku.city.yokohama.jp/cms_files_maibun/pr_brochure/my031-hi.pdf Maibun Yokohama No. 31 (Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群)]'' (March 20, 2015).
== See Also == * {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}} * {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} ({{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}) * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} * {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} * {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|1=Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97613655}} * {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|ja|横浜市指定文化財および地域文化財一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|1=List of Yokohama City Designated and Registered Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543021}}
