Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, [[:en:Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama]], [[:en:Yokohama|Yokohama]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with [[:en:Sakae-ku, Yokohama|Sakae-ku, Yokohama]], making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97636257]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]]
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]]
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|lt=Cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]]
[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|lt=Cave Tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Cave Tombs. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|lt=Cave Tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Cave Tomb Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).

[[File:Matsugasaki Tunnel Tomb.jpg|230px|One of the Matsugasaki Yokoanabos. Only a small portion of the tomb entrance is visible, buried under vegetation (photo taken May 5, 2020).|thumbnail]] {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=right|frame-width=230}}
'''Matsugasaki Yokoanabo Cluster''' (松ヶ崎横穴墓群) is a {{ill|cave tomb cluster|ja|横穴墓群遺跡|3=横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|lt=Yokoanabos|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} dating from the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, located in {{ill|Kōnandai, Yokohama|ja|港南台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai, Yokohama|1=Kōnandai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563061}}, {{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153968|lt=Kōnan-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|Yokohama|WD=Q38283|lt=Yokohama}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} and has been registered as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}} (Regional Historic Site).<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.townnews.co.jp/0112/2015/11/26/309854.html|title=港南台 横穴墓群が地域文化財に|website=Town News Kōnan & Sakae Ward Edition|date=2015-11-26|accessdate=2020-07-22}}</ref>
== Overview == The tombs are located on the slope of a hill south of {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|ja|神奈川県立横浜明朋高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Yokohama Meihō High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28686024}} (formerly {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|ja|神奈川県立港南台高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Kōnandai High School|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Konandai High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589129}}). Administratively, they fall under Kōnan-ku but are situated near the border with {{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|WD=Q1153916|lt=Sakae-ku, Yokohama}}, making them closely associated with the Sakae area. The tombs were once counted among the {{ill|Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|ja|港南台遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|1=Kōnandai Ruins Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109366328}}, and records indicate that eight tombs originally remained.<ref>{{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai'' (1976), pp. 156-158.</ref> However, the site is now overgrown with trees and underbrush, with only two or three entrances faintly visible.
The tombs feature a distinctive {{ill|Kamakura-style cave tomb|ja|鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura-style cave tombs|qq=}} design known as the {{ill|Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja|鍛冶ヶ谷式横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajigayatsu-style cave tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉型横穴墓|1=Kamakura-style cave tombs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97576952}}. This includes a {{ill|burial chamber|ja|玄室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial chamber|1=genshitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572272}} with a narrow, elongated trapezoidal floor, a semi-cylindrical ceiling, and a small chamber referred to as a "coffin chamber" (棺室) carved into the back wall.<ref>{{ill|Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|ja|埋蔵文化財センター (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|1=Yokohama City Archaeological Research Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929301}}, ''Maibun Yokohama 31'' (2015), pp. 1-3.</ref>
On November 13, 2015, the site was designated as a {{ill|Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|ja|横浜市登録地域文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Registered Cultural Property|qq=}}, under the category of "Regional Historic Sites."<ref name=yokohama>{{Cite web|language=ja|title=指定・登録文化財目録|publisher={{ill|Yokohama City Board of Education|ja|横浜市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama City Board of Education|1=Yokohama City Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543020}}|url=https://www.city.yokohama.lg.jp/kurashi/kyodo-manabi/bunkazai/bunya12000.files/0007_20191120.pdf|date=2020-05-29|accessdate=2020-07-24}}</ref>
== Published Works == * {{ill|Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja|鵠沼女子高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugenuma Girls' High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鵠沼高等学校|1=Kugenuma Girls' High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675787}} Geography and History Research Club, ''A Study on the Yokoanabo Clusters along the Kashio River in Totsuka Ward, Yokohama'' (March 20, 1964). * {{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja|神奈川県教育庁社会教育部文化財保護課|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, ''Kōnandai – Archaeological Survey Associated with the Kōnandai Land Readjustment Project (Kanagawa Prefecture Archaeological Survey Report 9)'' (March 1976). * Kōnan History Publishing Committee, ''History of Kōnan'' (October 1, 1979).
